medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Started noting headaches, lethargy and decline in mobility to the point where she was confined to a wheelchair in 2006. Underwent fourth ventricular cysto–pleural shunt insertion in April 2007. Initially well, but at 4 months following surgery she deteriorated with a left 7th nerve palsy, right 6th nerve palsy, tetraparesis, and difficulty in swallowing. A second shunt was placed in the new cyst in August 2007. After this second operation the patient did not improve and developed increasing weakness and more marked bulbar disturbance. The cysto–pleural shunt was clipped in December 2007. Despite this, her clinical state continued to deteriorate, and she required naso-enteric feeding and developed a marked tetraparesis.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Born with a myelomeningocele and had a ventriculo-peritoneal shunt inserted at the age of 2 weeks. Multiple shunt revisions, the last in 1985. An MRI in 1996 showed a normal fourth ventricle.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Left 7th nerve palsy, right 6th nerve palsy, tetraparesis, difficulty in swallowing suggestive of vagal nerve dysfunction.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Persisting cystic change at the cranio-cervical junction and T2 signal changes in the upper cervical cord
New and progressive changes in the medulla and cervical cord
Decreasing size of the known syrinx, and gradual resolution of the oedema in the brain stem and upper cervical canal

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Isolated fourth ventricle in Chiari II malformation
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with deterioration in motor function, headaches, lethargy, and decline in mobility. MRI revealed an isolated fourth ventricle. The reasoning includes the obstruction of the outlet foraminae of the fourth ventricle, possibly due to scarring, leading to ventricular enlargement. The patient's clinical state and imaging findings supported the diagnosis.
